history.label: 17th century
history.text: Billund is recorded as a settlement with origins in the 17th century in central Jutland.
The carpentry business later bought by Ole Kirk Christiansen was founded in Billund.
Ole Kirk Christiansen bought Billund Machine Joinery and Carpentry Business, the workshop that later led to Lego.
Christiansen named his toy business Lego, anchoring the company identity that would reshape the town.
Billund Airport opened to the public after growing from a private runway linked to the Lego company.
Legoland Billund opened as the first Legoland park, turning the town into a major family tourism base.
